theme: Student Visits Professor About Research Paper

sentences.english: Listen to a conversation between a student and a professor.
sentences.korean: 학생과 교수 사이의 대화를 들어보세요.

sentences.english: Professor Hayes? Do you have a few minutes? I wanted to talk about my research paper.
sentences.korean: 헤이즈 교수님? 잠깐 시간 되세요? 제 연구 논문에 대해 이야기하고 싶었어요.

sentences.english: Of course, come in. How's it going?
sentences.korean: 물론이지, 들어와. 어떻게 되어가고 있어?

sentences.english: Well, that's sort of the issue. I started out writing about the economic causes of World War I, but the more I research, the more I feel like I'm being pulled toward the political causes instead.
sentences.korean: 음, 그게 좀 문제예요. 처음에는 1차 세계대전의 경제적 원인에 대해 쓰기 시작했는데, 연구할수록 정치적 원인 쪽으로 끌리는 느낌이에요.

sentences.english: Is it okay to change my focus this late?
sentences.korean: 이렇게 늦게 초점을 바꿔도 될까요?

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Student Visits Professor About Research Paper

theme: Student Visits Professor About Research Paper

sentences.english: How late are we talking? When is your draft due?
sentences.korean: 얼마나 늦은 거지? 초안 마감이 언제야?

sentences.english: Two weeks from Friday.
sentences.korean: 금요일로부터 2주 후요.

sentences.english: That's actually not as tight as I feared.
sentences.korean: 사실 내가 걱정한 것만큼 빠듯하지는 않네.

sentences.english: Look — changing your focus partway through is more common than students think.
sentences.korean: 봐 — 중간에 초점을 바꾸는 건 학생들이 생각하는 것보다 더 흔해.

sentences.english: The question is how much of your existing work you can salvage. Have you done substantial research on the political side already?
sentences.korean: 문제는 기존 작업을 얼마나 살릴 수 있느냐야. 정치적 측면 연구를 이미 상당히 했어?

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Student Visits Professor About Research Paper

theme: Student Visits Professor About Research Paper

sentences.english: Yes, actually. I've read a lot about the alliance systems and the role of nationalism.
sentences.korean: 네, 사실 동맹 체제와 민족주의의 역할에 대해 많이 읽었어요.

sentences.english: I got kind of pulled into it when I was reading about the July Crisis.
sentences.korean: 7월 위기에 대해 읽다가 빠져들게 됐어요.

sentences.english: That's a very rich area. The July Crisis alone could be a paper by itself.
sentences.korean: 아주 풍부한 분야야. 7월 위기만으로도 논문 하나가 될 수 있어.

sentences.english: What specifically interests you?
sentences.korean: 구체적으로 무엇에 관심이 있어?

sentences.english: I'm most interested in how quickly the decisions escalated — how each country felt trapped by its commitments to allies, even when their leaders seemed to want to avoid war.
sentences.korean: 가장 관심 있는 건 결정이 얼마나 빠르게 격화됐는지예요 — 지도자들이 전쟁을 피하고 싶어 하는 것 같았는데도 각국이 동맹 의무에 갇혀 있다고 느꼈다는 거요.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Student Visits Professor About Research Paper

theme: Student Visits Professor About Research Paper

sentences.english: That's actually a well-developed historiographical debate.
sentences.korean: 그건 사실 잘 발전된 역사학적 논쟁이야.

sentences.english: You might want to look at Christopher Clark's work — he wrote about how the leaders sleepwalked into the war without fully understanding the consequences.
sentences.korean: 크리스토퍼 클라크의 연구를 살펴봐 — 지도자들이 결과를 제대로 이해하지 못한 채 전쟁에 몽유병처럼 빠져들었다고 썼어.

sentences.english: But there are historians who strongly disagree with that thesis.
sentences.korean: 하지만 그 주장에 강하게 반대하는 역사학자들도 있어.

sentences.english: Oh, that's interesting. So I could frame it as a comparison between different historical interpretations?
sentences.korean: 오, 흥미롭네요. 그러면 여러 역사적 해석의 비교로 구성할 수 있겠네요?

sentences.english: Exactly. Instead of just stating what happened, you'd be engaging with the historiography — which is more sophisticated for a paper at this level.
sentences.korean: 맞아. 무슨 일이 있었는지 단순히 서술하는 대신 역사서술학을 다루는 거야 — 이 수준 논문으로는 더 정교하지.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Student Visits Professor About Research Paper

theme: Student Visits Professor About Research Paper

sentences.english: That would actually impress me more than a straightforward narrative.
sentences.korean: 그게 사실 단순한 서사보다 더 인상적일 거야.

sentences.english: Okay. That feels more exciting to write, honestly.
sentences.korean: 좋아요. 솔직히 그게 쓰기에 더 흥미로울 것 같아요.

sentences.english: Good. The best papers come from genuine curiosity.
sentences.korean: 좋아. 최고의 논문은 진정한 호기심에서 나오거든.

sentences.english: My advice: outline the two or three key historiographical positions first, then build your argument for which interpretation you find most convincing and why.
sentences.korean: 내 조언은 먼저 2~3개의 핵심 역사학적 입장을 개요로 잡고, 어떤 해석이 가장 설득력 있는지 그 이유와 함께 논증을 구축하는 거야.

sentences.english: Use primary sources wherever possible to support your view.
sentences.korean: 가능한 한 1차 자료를 사용해서 네 관점을 뒷받침해.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Student Visits Professor About Research Paper

theme: Student Visits Professor About Research Paper

sentences.english: Should I change my thesis statement before I do the outline?
sentences.korean: 개요 잡기 전에 논지를 바꿔야 할까요?

sentences.english: Write a tentative thesis — something flexible — and let it develop as you outline.
sentences.korean: 잠정적인 논지를 써봐 — 유연한 것으로 — 그리고 개요를 잡으면서 발전시켜.

sentences.english: Don't lock yourself in too early. The thesis should come from your argument, not the other way around.
sentences.korean: 너무 일찍 정하지 마. 논지는 네 논증에서 나와야 하지, 그 반대가 아니야.

sentences.english: That makes sense. Thank you — this actually made me feel a lot better about the paper.
sentences.korean: 이해됐어요. 감사합니다 — 이 대화 덕분에 논문에 대해 한결 나은 기분이 들어요.

sentences.english: Good luck. And send me your outline when it's ready. I'll give you some quick feedback before you dive into the full draft.
sentences.korean: 행운을 빌어. 그리고 개요가 되면 보내줘. 전체 초안 작성 전에 빠른 피드백 줄게.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Student Visits Professor About Research Paper
